# list-toolkit

> A zero-dependency JavaScript library providing efficient list-based data structures: doubly and singly linked circular lists, caches, heaps, queues, stacks, splay trees, skip lists, and null-terminated list utilities.

- NPM: https://npmjs.org/package/list-toolkit
- GitHub: https://github.com/uhop/list-toolkit
- Wiki: https://github.com/uhop/list-toolkit/wiki
- License: BSD-3-Clause
- Runtime: Node.js, Bun, Deno, browsers
- Module system: ESM (CJS supported via Node.js 22+ native ESM loader)
- TypeScript: built-in `.d.ts` declarations for all modules

## Installation

```bash
npm install list-toolkit
```

## Quick start

```js
import ValueList from 'list-toolkit/value-list.js';

const list = ValueList.from([1, 2, 3]);
for (const value of list) console.log(value); // 1, 2, 3

list.pushBack(4);
list.pushFront(0);
console.log(list.popFront()); // 0
```

```js
import List from 'list-toolkit/list.js';

// Node-based: link properties added directly to your objects
const a = {name: 'Alice'}, b = {name: 'Bob'};
const people = List.from([a, b]);
for (const node of people) console.log(node.name); // Alice, Bob

// Custom link names allow objects in multiple lists
const team = List.from([a, b], {nextName: 'teamNext', prevName: 'teamPrev'});
```

---

## Key concepts

- All lists are **circular** ‚Äî the last node links back to the head.
- **Hosted lists** use a `HeadNode` sentinel. **External (headless) lists** point into an existing circular list.
- **Value lists** wrap values in `ValueNode` containers. **Node lists** use link properties directly on user objects.
- **DLL** (doubly linked) uses `nextName`/`prevName` (default: `'next'`/`'prev'`). **SLL** (singly linked) uses `nextName` (default: `'next'`).
- Custom link names allow the same object to participate in multiple lists simultaneously.
- **Pointers** (`Ptr`) provide safe iteration with insert/remove during traversal.
- Method aliases (e.g., `popFront` ‚Üí `pop`) are created via `addAlias`/`addAliases`.

---

## Module: List (`list-toolkit/list.js`)

Hosted node-based doubly linked circular list. Link properties are added directly to user objects.

```js
import List from 'list-toolkit/list.js';
```

### Constructor

```js
new List({nextName = 'next', prevName = 'prev'} = {})
```

### Static methods

- `List.from(values, options)` ‚Äî create a list from an iterable.
- `List.fromRange(range, options)` ‚Äî create from a `{from, to}` range.
- `List.fromExtList(extList)` ‚Äî create from an `ExtList`.

### Properties

- `isEmpty` ‚Äî true if list has no nodes.
- `isOne` ‚Äî true if list has exactly one node.
- `isOneOrEmpty` ‚Äî true if list has zero or one node.
- `front` ‚Äî first node.
- `back` ‚Äî last node.
- `frontPtr` ‚Äî `Ptr` to first node.
- `backPtr` ‚Äî `Ptr` to last node.
- `range` ‚Äî `{from, to, list}` or null if empty.
- `head` ‚Äî the sentinel `HeadNode`.

### Methods

- `pushFront(value)` ‚Äî add node to front. Returns `Ptr`. Alias: `push`.
- `pushBack(value)` ‚Äî add node to back. Returns `Ptr`.
- `popFrontNode()` ‚Äî remove and return front node. Aliases: `popFront`, `pop`.
- `popBackNode()` ‚Äî remove and return back node. Alias: `popBack`.
- `pushFrontNode(nodeOrPtr)` ‚Äî add existing node to front. Returns `Ptr`.
- `pushBackNode(nodeOrPtr)` ‚Äî add existing node to back. Returns `Ptr`.
- `appendFront(list)` ‚Äî move all nodes from another list to front.
- `appendBack(list)` ‚Äî move all nodes from another list to back. Alias: `append`.
- `moveToFront(nodeOrPtr)` ‚Äî move a node to front.
- `moveToBack(nodeOrPtr)` ‚Äî move a node to back.
- `removeNode(nodeOrPtr)` ‚Äî remove a specific node.
- `removeRange(range, drop)` ‚Äî remove a range of nodes.
- `extractRange(range)` ‚Äî extract a range into a new list.
- `extractBy(condition)` ‚Äî extract nodes matching condition into a new list.
- `reverse()` ‚Äî reverse the list in place.
- `sort(lessFn)` ‚Äî merge sort in place. `lessFn(a, b)` returns true if a < b.
- `clear(drop)` ‚Äî remove all nodes.
- `getLength()` ‚Äî count nodes (O(n)).
- `makePtr(node)` ‚Äî create a `Ptr` to a node.
- `validateRange(range)` ‚Äî check if range is valid.
- `releaseRawList()` ‚Äî detach nodes as a raw circular list.
- `releaseNTList()` ‚Äî detach nodes as a null-terminated list `{head, tail}`.

### Iterators

- `[Symbol.iterator]()` ‚Äî iterates over nodes.
- `getNodeIterator(range)` ‚Äî node iterator over optional range. Alias: `getIterator`.
- `getPtrIterator(range)` ‚Äî `Ptr` iterator (safe for insert/remove).
- `getReverseNodeIterator(range)` ‚Äî reverse node iterator. Alias: `getReverseIterator`.
- `getReversePtrIterator(range)` ‚Äî reverse `Ptr` iterator.

---

## Module: ValueList (`list-toolkit/value-list.js`)

Hosted value-based doubly linked circular list. Extends `List`. Wraps values in `ValueNode` containers.

```js
import ValueList from 'list-toolkit/value-list.js';
```

### Static methods

- `ValueList.from(values, options)` ‚Äî create from iterable.

### Additional/overridden methods

- `pushFront(value)` ‚Äî wraps value in `ValueNode`, adds to front.
- `pushBack(value)` ‚Äî wraps value in `ValueNode`, adds to back.
- `popFront()` ‚Äî remove front node, return its `.value`. Alias: `pop`.
- `popBack()` ‚Äî remove back node, return its `.value`.
- `clone()` ‚Äî deep clone the list.

### Iterators

- `[Symbol.iterator]()` ‚Äî iterates over **values** (not nodes).
- `getValueIterator(range)` ‚Äî value iterator. Alias: `getIterator`.
- `getReverseValueIterator(range)` ‚Äî reverse value iterator. Alias: `getReverseIterator`.
- `getNodeIterator(range)` ‚Äî node iterator (inherited from `List`).

---

## Module: SList (`list-toolkit/slist.js`)

Hosted node-based singly linked circular list. Same API pattern as `List` but without `prev` links.

```js
import SList from 'list-toolkit/slist.js';
```

Constructor: `new SList({nextName = 'next'} = {})`

Same methods as `List` where applicable. Key differences:
- `back` is O(1) via a cached `last` pointer (kept in sync by all mutations).
- No reverse iterators.
- No `popBack` ‚Äî removing the last node requires traversal in an SLL.

---

## Module: ValueSList (`list-toolkit/value-slist.js`)

Hosted value-based singly linked circular list. Extends `SList`. Same relationship as `ValueList` to `List`.

```js
import ValueSList from 'list-toolkit/value-slist.js';
```

---

## Module: ExtList (`list-toolkit/ext-list.js`)

External (headless) node-based doubly linked list. Points into an existing circular list without a sentinel.

```js
import ExtList from 'list-toolkit/ext-list.js';
```

Constructor: `new ExtList(head = null, {nextName = 'next', prevName = 'prev'} = {})`

### Properties

- `isEmpty` ‚Äî true if no head.
- `isOne` ‚Äî true if single node.
- `front` ‚Äî head node.
- `back` ‚Äî node before head.
- `head` ‚Äî current head node.

### Methods

- `attach(head)` ‚Äî set head node. Returns old head.
- `detach()` ‚Äî clear head. Returns old head.
- `next()` ‚Äî advance head to next node.
- `prev()` ‚Äî advance head to previous node.
- Plus node manipulation methods similar to `List`.

---

## Module: ExtValueList (`list-toolkit/ext-value-list.js`)

External (headless) value-based doubly linked list.

```js
import ExtValueList from 'list-toolkit/ext-value-list.js';
```

---

## Module: ExtSList (`list-toolkit/ext-slist.js`)

External (headless) node-based singly linked list.

```js
import ExtSList from 'list-toolkit/ext-slist.js';
```

---

## Module: ExtValueSList (`list-toolkit/ext-value-slist.js`)

External (headless) value-based singly linked list.

```js
import ExtValueSList from 'list-toolkit/ext-value-slist.js';
```

---

## Module: Ptr (`list-toolkit/list/ptr.js`)

Pointer for safe DLL traversal with insert/remove during iteration.

```js
// Usually obtained from list methods, not imported directly
const ptr = list.frontPtr;
const ptr = list.makePtr(node);
for (const ptr of list.getPtrIterator()) { /* ... */ }
```

### Properties

- `list` ‚Äî the owning list.
- `node` ‚Äî the current node.
- `isHead` ‚Äî true if pointing at the sentinel.
- `nextNode` ‚Äî next node.
- `prevNode` ‚Äî previous node.

### Methods

- `next()` ‚Äî advance to next node. Returns `this`.
- `prev()` ‚Äî advance to previous node. Returns `this`.
- `clone()` ‚Äî create a copy of this pointer.
- `removeCurrent()` ‚Äî remove current node, advance to next. Returns removed node.
- `addBefore(value)` ‚Äî insert value before current. Returns `Ptr` to new node.
- `addAfter(value)` ‚Äî insert value after current. Returns `Ptr` to new node.
- `addNodeBefore(node)` ‚Äî insert existing node before current.
- `addNodeAfter(node)` ‚Äî insert existing node after current.
- `insertBefore(list)` ‚Äî splice entire list before current.
- `insertAfter(list)` ‚Äî splice entire list after current.

---

## Module: CacheLRU (`list-toolkit/cache.js`)

Least recently used cache. Default export of `cache.js`.

```js
import CacheLRU from 'list-toolkit/cache.js';
// or: import {CacheLRU} from 'list-toolkit/cache/cache-lru.js';
```

### Constructor

```js
new CacheLRU(capacity = 10)
```

### Properties

- `isEmpty` ‚Äî true if cache is empty.
- `size` ‚Äî number of entries.
- `capacity` ‚Äî maximum entries.

### Methods

- `has(key)` ‚Äî check if key exists.
- `find(key)` ‚Äî get value by key (marks as recently used). Alias: `get`.
- `register(key, value)` ‚Äî add or update entry. Aliases: `add`, `set`.
- `remove(key)` ‚Äî remove entry. Alias: `delete`.
- `clear()` ‚Äî remove all entries.
- `[Symbol.iterator]()` ‚Äî iterate over entries.
- `getReverseIterator()` ‚Äî reverse iterator.

---

## Module: CacheFIFO (`list-toolkit/cache/cache-fifo.js`)

First in first out cache. Same API as `CacheLRU`.

```js
import {CacheFIFO} from 'list-toolkit/cache/cache-fifo.js';
```

---

## Module: CacheLFU (`list-toolkit/cache/cache-lfu.js`)

Least frequently used cache. Same API as `CacheLRU`.

```js
import {CacheLFU} from 'list-toolkit/cache/cache-lfu.js';
```

---

## Module: CacheRandom (`list-toolkit/cache/cache-random.js`)

Random eviction cache. Same API as `CacheLRU`.

```js
import {CacheRandom} from 'list-toolkit/cache/cache-random.js';
```

---

## Module: Cache decorator (`list-toolkit/cache.js`)

Decorator to cache function/method results based on the first argument.

```js
import {cacheDecorator} from 'list-toolkit/cache.js';
import {decorateFn, decorate, decorateMethod, getCache} from 'list-toolkit/cache/decorator.js';
```

- `cacheDecorator(object, key, cache?)` ‚Äî decorate a method on an object.
- `decorateFn(fn, cache)` ‚Äî wrap a function with caching.
- `decorate(object, key, cache)` ‚Äî decorate via property descriptor.
- `decorateMethod(object, key, cache)` ‚Äî decorate by direct assignment.
- `getCache(object, key)` ‚Äî retrieve the cache from a decorated property.

---

## Module: MinHeap (`list-toolkit/heap.js`)

Array-based binary min-heap. Default export of `heap.js`.

```js
import MinHeap from 'list-toolkit/heap.js';
// or: import {MinHeap} from 'list-toolkit/heap/min-heap.js';
```

### Constructor

```js
new MinHeap(options?, ...initialData)
```

Options: `{less, equal, compare}`. If `compare` is provided, `less` is derived from it.

### Properties

- `isEmpty` ‚Äî true if heap is empty.
- `length` ‚Äî number of elements.
- `top` ‚Äî minimum element (without removing).
- `array` ‚Äî the underlying array.

### Instance methods

- `peek()` ‚Äî alias for `top`.
- `push(value)` ‚Äî add element. Returns `this`.
- `pop()` ‚Äî remove and return minimum.
- `pushPop(value)` ‚Äî push then pop (optimized).
- `replaceTop(value)` ‚Äî replace minimum with value, re-heapify.
- `has(value)` ‚Äî check if value exists.
- `findIndex(value)` ‚Äî find index of value.
- `remove(value)` ‚Äî remove a value.
- `removeByIndex(index)` ‚Äî remove by array index.
- `replace(value, newValue)` ‚Äî replace a value.
- `replaceByIndex(index, newValue)` ‚Äî replace by index.
- `updateTop()` ‚Äî re-heapify after mutating top element.
- `updateByIndex(index, isDecreased)` ‚Äî re-heapify after mutation.
- `clear()` ‚Äî remove all elements.
- `merge(...heapsOrArrays)` ‚Äî merge other heaps/arrays into this one.
- `clone()` ‚Äî shallow clone.
- `releaseSorted()` ‚Äî extract sorted array, clear heap.

### Static methods (operate on raw arrays)

- `MinHeap.build(array, less?)` ‚Äî heapify an array in place.
- `MinHeap.push(heapArray, item, less?)` ‚Äî push to heap array.
- `MinHeap.pop(heapArray, less?)` ‚Äî pop from heap array.
- `MinHeap.pushPop(heapArray, item, less?)` ‚Äî push then pop.
- `MinHeap.replaceTop(heapArray, item, less?)` ‚Äî replace top.
- `MinHeap.has(heapArray, item, equal?)` ‚Äî check existence.
- `MinHeap.findIndex(heapArray, item, equal?)` ‚Äî find index.
- `MinHeap.remove(heapArray, item, less?, equal?)` ‚Äî remove item.
- `MinHeap.removeByIndex(heapArray, index, less?)` ‚Äî remove by index.
- `MinHeap.replace(heapArray, item, newItem, less?, equal?)` ‚Äî replace.
- `MinHeap.replaceByIndex(heapArray, index, newItem, less?)` ‚Äî replace by index.
- `MinHeap.updateByIndex(heapArray, index, isDecreased, less?)` ‚Äî re-heapify.
- `MinHeap.sort(heapArray, less?)` ‚Äî heap sort in place.
- `MinHeap.from(array, options?)` ‚Äî create heap from array.

---

## Module: IndexedHeap (`list-toolkit/heap/indexed-heap.js`)

Array-based binary min-heap that stores each element's position on the element itself under a configurable property name (intrusive index). O(1) `has`/`findIndex`; O(log n) `update`/`remove`/`replace` by handle ‚Äî decrease-key with no scans. Elements must be objects.

```js
import IndexedHeap from 'list-toolkit/heap/indexed-heap.js';

const heap = new IndexedHeap({less: (a, b) => a.deadline < b.deadline});
const task = {deadline: 150};
heap.push(task);
task.deadline = 42;
heap.update(task); // reschedule by handle, O(log n)
heap.remove(task); // cancel by handle, O(log n)
```

### Constructor

```js
new IndexedHeap({less?, compare?, indexName = 'heapIndex'}?, ...heapsOrIterables)
```

If `compare` is provided, `less` is derived. `indexName` is the property (string or symbol) written on elements; use distinct names to keep one object in several heaps. Heap arguments are drained (merged in).

### Properties

- `isEmpty` ‚Äî true if the heap is empty.
- `length` ‚Äî number of elements.
- `top` ‚Äî minimum element (without removing).
- `array` ‚Äî the underlying array (a valid binary heap).
- `indexName` ‚Äî the index property name.

### Methods

- `peek()` ‚Äî alias for `top`.
- `push(value)` ‚Äî add an element. Returns `this`.
- `pop()` ‚Äî remove and return the minimum.
- `pushPop(value)` ‚Äî push then pop (optimized).
- `replaceTop(value)` ‚Äî replace the minimum, re-heapify.
- `findIndex(value)` ‚Äî element's position, or -1. O(1).
- `has(value)` ‚Äî membership check. O(1).
- `update(value, isDecreased?)` ‚Äî re-heapify after mutating the element's priority (decrease-key); direction auto-detected when omitted.
- `updateTop()` ‚Äî re-heapify after mutating the top element.
- `remove(value)` ‚Äî remove by handle. O(log n).
- `removeByIndex(index)` ‚Äî remove by position.
- `replace(value, newValue)` / `replaceByIndex(index, newValue)` ‚Äî replace, re-heapify.
- `merge(...args)` ‚Äî merge heaps/arrays/iterables, rebuild in O(n). Heap arguments are drained.
- `build()` ‚Äî heapify `array` in place, restamp indices. O(n).
- `clear()` ‚Äî remove all elements.
- `releaseSorted()` ‚Äî heap-sort in place (reverse `less` order), detach and return the array; indices reset.
- `IndexedHeap.from(values, options?)` ‚Äî create from an iterable in O(n); an array is adopted.

No `clone()`: two heaps cannot track the same elements under one index name.

---

## Module: LeftistHeap (`list-toolkit/heap/leftist-heap.js`)

Node-based merge heap using the leftist property. Efficient O(log n) merge.

```js
import {LeftistHeap} from 'list-toolkit/heap/leftist-heap.js';
```

### Constructor

```js
new LeftistHeap(options?, ...heapsToMerge)
```

### Methods

- `push(value)` ‚Äî add element. Returns `this`.
- `pop()` ‚Äî remove and return minimum.
- `pushPop(value)` ‚Äî push then pop (optimized).
- `replaceTop(value)` ‚Äî replace minimum.
- `merge(...heaps)` ‚Äî merge other heaps (destructive to arguments).
- `clone()` ‚Äî deep clone.
- `clear()` ‚Äî remove all.
- `LeftistHeap.from(array, options?)` ‚Äî create from array.

Properties: `isEmpty`, `length` (alias: `size`), `top`, `peek()`.

---

## Module: SkewHeap (`list-toolkit/heap/skew-heap.js`)

Node-based merge heap. Simpler than leftist, same asymptotic complexity (amortized).

```js
import {SkewHeap} from 'list-toolkit/heap/skew-heap.js';
```

Same API as `LeftistHeap`.

---

## Module: Queue (`list-toolkit/queue.js`)

FIFO queue adapter wrapping a `ValueList`.

```js
import Queue from 'list-toolkit/queue.js';
```

### Constructor

```js
new Queue(underlyingList = new ValueList())
```

### Properties

- `isEmpty` ‚Äî true if empty.
- `size` ‚Äî number of elements.
- `top` ‚Äî front element value.

### Methods

- `peek()` ‚Äî return front value.
- `add(value)` ‚Äî add to back. Aliases: `push`, `pushBack`, `enqueue`.
- `remove()` ‚Äî remove from front. Aliases: `pop`, `popFront`, `dequeue`.
- `addValues(values)` ‚Äî add multiple values.
- `clear()` ‚Äî remove all.
- `[Symbol.iterator]()` ‚Äî iterate over values.
- `getReverseIterator()` ‚Äî reverse iterator.

---

## Module: Stack (`list-toolkit/stack.js`)

LIFO stack adapter wrapping a `ValueList`.

```js
import Stack from 'list-toolkit/stack.js';
```

### Constructor

```js
new Stack(UnderlyingList = ValueList)
```

### Properties

- `isEmpty` ‚Äî true if empty.
- `size` ‚Äî number of elements.
- `top` ‚Äî top element value.

### Methods

- `peek()` ‚Äî return top value.
- `push(value)` ‚Äî push to top. Alias: `pushFront`.
- `pop()` ‚Äî remove and return top value.
- `pushValues(values)` ‚Äî push multiple values.
- `clear()` ‚Äî remove all.
- `[Symbol.iterator]()` ‚Äî iterate over values.
- `getReverseIterator()` ‚Äî reverse iterator.

---

## Module: SplayTree (`list-toolkit/tree/splay-tree.js`)

Self-adjusting binary search tree. Frequently accessed elements move to the root.

```js
import SplayTree from 'list-toolkit/tree/splay-tree.js';
```

### Constructor

```js
new SplayTree({less?, compare?} = {})
```

If `compare` is provided, `less` is derived. Default `less`: `(a, b) => a < b`.

### Properties

- `isEmpty` ‚Äî true if tree is empty.
- `length` (alias: `size`) ‚Äî number of nodes.
- `root` ‚Äî root `SplayTreeNode` or null.

### Methods

- `insert(value)` ‚Äî insert value (no duplicates). Returns `this`.
- `find(value)` ‚Äî find node by value. Returns `SplayTreeNode` or null.
- `promote(value)` ‚Äî find and splay to root. Returns node or null.
- `splay(node)` ‚Äî splay a node to root. Returns `this`.
- `remove(value)` ‚Äî remove by value. Returns `this`.
- `getMin()` ‚Äî return node with minimum value.
- `getMax()` ‚Äî return node with maximum value.
- `splitMaxTree(value)` ‚Äî split: values > value go to new tree. Returns new `SplayTree`.
- `join(tree)` ‚Äî merge another tree into this one (destructive to argument).
- `clear()` ‚Äî remove all nodes.
- `[Symbol.iterator]()` ‚Äî in-order iteration over values.
- `getReverseIterator()` ‚Äî reverse in-order iteration.
- `SplayTree.from(values, options?)` ‚Äî create from iterable.

### SplayTreeNode

- `value` ‚Äî the stored value.
- `left`, `right`, `parent` ‚Äî child and parent pointers.
- `getMin()` ‚Äî find minimum in subtree.
- `getMax()` ‚Äî find maximum in subtree.

---

## Module: SkipList (`list-toolkit/skip-list.js`)

Probabilistic ordered container: layered singly linked lanes over a doubly linked bottom lane. Expected O(log n) search/insert/remove; reads do not mutate the structure (contrast with SplayTree).

```js
import SkipList from 'list-toolkit/skip-list.js';
```

### Constructor

```js
new SkipList({less?, compare?, p = 0.5, maxLevel = 32, random = Math.random} = {})
```

If `compare` is provided, `less` is derived. Default `less`: `(a, b) => a < b`. `random` can be injected for deterministic behavior.

### Properties

- `isEmpty` ‚Äî true if the list is empty.
- `size` (alias: `length`) ‚Äî number of elements.
- `head` ‚Äî head sentinel `SkipListNode` (its `next` array is the lane tower).
- `last` ‚Äî last node, or null if empty.

### Methods

- `insert(value)` ‚Äî insert a value (duplicates ignored). Returns `this`.
- `find(value)` ‚Äî find node by value. Returns `SkipListNode` or null.
- `has(value)` ‚Äî check presence. Returns boolean.
- `remove(value)` ‚Äî remove a value (missing ignored). Returns `this`.
- `floor(value)` ‚Äî node with the greatest value ‚â§ `value`, or null.
- `ceil(value)` ‚Äî node with the smallest value ‚â• `value`, or null.
- `getMin()` ‚Äî first node, or null. O(1).
- `getMax()` ‚Äî last node, or null. O(1).
- `popFront()` ‚Äî remove and return the minimum value, or undefined. O(1) expected.
- `clear()` ‚Äî remove all elements.
- `[Symbol.iterator]()` ‚Äî values in ascending order.
- `getIterator({from?, to?})` ‚Äî values within the inclusive range.
- `getNodeIterator({from?, to?})` ‚Äî nodes within the inclusive range.
- `getReverseIterator()` ‚Äî values in descending order.
- `SkipList.from(values, options?)` ‚Äî create from an iterable.

### SkipListNode

- `value` ‚Äî the stored value.
- `next` ‚Äî array of forward pointers (index 0 is the bottom lane).
- `prev` ‚Äî previous node on the bottom lane, or null.
- `level` ‚Äî tower height.

---

## Module: list-utils (`list-toolkit/list-utils.js`)

Utility functions for working with lists.

```js
import {pushValuesFront, pushValuesBack, findNodeBy, removeNodeBy} from 'list-toolkit/list-utils.js';
```

- `isValidList(list)` ‚Äî validate DLL circular structure.
- `isValidSList(list)` ‚Äî validate SLL circular structure.
- `pushValuesFront(list, values)` ‚Äî push multiple values to front.
- `pushValuesBack(list, values)` ‚Äî push multiple values to back.
- `appendValuesFront(list, values)` ‚Äî append values to front (uses `appendFront` if compatible).
- `appendValuesBack(list, values)` ‚Äî append values to back (uses `appendBack` if compatible).
- `addValuesBefore(ptr, values)` ‚Äî add values before pointer.
- `addValuesAfter(ptr, values)` ‚Äî add values after pointer.
- `insertValuesBefore(ptr, values)` ‚Äî insert values before pointer (bulk if compatible).
- `insertValuesAfter(ptr, values)` ‚Äî insert values after pointer (bulk if compatible).
- `findNodeBy(list, condition)` ‚Äî find first node matching condition.
- `findPtrBy(list, condition)` ‚Äî find first `Ptr` matching condition.
- `removeNodeBy(list, condition)` ‚Äî remove first node matching condition.
- `backPusher(ExtListClass, options)` ‚Äî create adapter for building lists by pushing to back.
- `frontPusher(ExtListClass, options)` ‚Äî create adapter for building lists by pushing to front.

---

## Module: nt-utils (`list-toolkit/nt-utils.js`)

Utilities for null-terminated lists. Convert between NT and circular formats.

```js
import {makeListFromNTList, makeNTListFromList} from 'list-toolkit/nt-utils.js';
```

- `isNTList(head, {nextName?})` ‚Äî check if list is null-terminated.
- `getNTListTail(head, {nextName?})` ‚Äî find tail of NT list.
- `getNTListHead(node, {prevName?})` ‚Äî find head of NT list (via prev links).
- `getNTListLength(head, {nextName?})` ‚Äî count nodes in NT list.
- `makeListFromNTList(node, {nextName?, prevName?})` ‚Äî convert NT DLL to circular. Returns `{head, tail}`.
- `makeSListFromNTList(head, {nextName?})` ‚Äî convert NT SLL to circular. Returns `{head, tail}`.
- `makeNTListFromList(head, {nextName?, prevName?})` ‚Äî convert circular DLL to NT. Returns `{head, tail}`.
- `makeNTListFromSList(head, {nextName?})` ‚Äî convert circular SLL to NT. Returns `{head, tail}`.
- `makeNTListFromSListFast(head, {nextName?})` ‚Äî fast convert (head becomes tail). Returns `{head, tail}`.

---

## Module: meta-utils (`list-toolkit/meta-utils.js`)

Internal utilities used throughout the library. Also useful for extending it.

```js
import {addAlias, addAliases, copyOptions} from 'list-toolkit/meta-utils.js';
```

- `addAlias(object, name, aliases, force?)` ‚Äî create method aliases.
- `addAliases(object, dict, force?)` ‚Äî create multiple aliases. Dict: `{methodName: 'alias1, alias2'}`.
- `copyOptions(target, pattern, ...sources)` ‚Äî copy known keys from sources to target.
- `copyDescriptors(target, source, names, force?)` ‚Äî copy property descriptors.
- `mapIterator(iterator, callbackFn)` ‚Äî map over an iterator.
- `normalizeIterator(iterator)` ‚Äî ensure `[Symbol.iterator]` is present.
- Name-casing utilities: `capitalize`, `toCamelCase`, `fromCamelCase`, `toPascalCase`, `toSnakeCase`, `toKebabCase`, etc.

---

## Common patterns

### Creating and iterating a value list

```js
import ValueList from 'list-toolkit/value-list.js';

const list = ValueList.from([10, 20, 30]);
for (const value of list) console.log(value); // 10, 20, 30

list.pushBack(40);
list.pushFront(0);
console.log(list.popFront()); // 0
console.log(list.getLength()); // 4
```

### Node-based list with custom link names

```js
import List from 'list-toolkit/list.js';

const a = {id: 1}, b = {id: 2}, c = {id: 3};
const list1 = List.from([a, b, c]);
const list2 = List.from([a, c], {nextName: 'n2', prevName: 'p2'});

// a is now in both lists simultaneously
for (const node of list1) console.log(node.id); // 1, 2, 3
for (const node of list2) console.log(node.id); // 1, 3
```

### Safe iteration with Ptr (insert/remove during traversal)

```js
import List from 'list-toolkit/list.js';

const list = List.from([{v: 1}, {v: 2}, {v: 3}, {v: 4}]);
for (const ptr of list.getPtrIterator()) {
  if (ptr.node.v % 2 === 0) ptr.removeCurrent();
}
// list now contains: {v: 1}, {v: 3}
```

### LRU cache

```js
import CacheLRU from 'list-toolkit/cache.js';

const cache = new CacheLRU(100);
cache.set('key1', 'value1');
cache.set('key2', 'value2');
console.log(cache.get('key1')); // 'value1'
console.log(cache.has('key3')); // false
cache.delete('key2');
```

### Min-heap as priority queue

```js
import MinHeap from 'list-toolkit/heap.js';

const heap = new MinHeap();
heap.push(5).push(1).push(3);
console.log(heap.top);  // 1
console.log(heap.pop()); // 1
console.log(heap.pop()); // 3

// With custom comparator
const maxHeap = new MinHeap({less: (a, b) => a > b});
maxHeap.push(1).push(5).push(3);
console.log(maxHeap.pop()); // 5
```

### Splay tree

```js
import SplayTree from 'list-toolkit/tree/splay-tree.js';

const tree = SplayTree.from([5, 3, 7, 1, 4]);
console.log(tree.find(3)?.value); // 3
tree.insert(6);
tree.remove(1);
for (const value of tree) console.log(value); // 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
```

### Queue and Stack

```js
import Queue from 'list-toolkit/queue.js';
import Stack from 'list-toolkit/stack.js';

const q = new Queue();
q.enqueue(1).enqueue(2).enqueue(3);
console.log(q.dequeue()); // 1

const s = new Stack();
s.push(1).push(2).push(3);
console.log(s.pop()); // 3
```

### Converting null-terminated lists

```js
import {makeListFromNTList, makeNTListFromList} from 'list-toolkit/nt-utils.js';

// Convert an existing NT doubly-linked list to circular
const head = {value: 1, next: {value: 2, next: null, prev: null}, prev: null};
head.next.prev = head;
const {head: circHead} = makeListFromNTList(head);

// Convert back
const {head: ntHead, tail: ntTail} = makeNTListFromList(circHead);
```
